title: solidago hirtella
intro: Solidago hirtella, also known as rough-leaved goldenrod, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is native to the eastern United States and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's flowers and leaves have been used to make herbal preparations to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Solidago hirtella can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and relieving respiratory issues.
